generated_text_by_instance.text: The Parla Sonic 3.5 is a discontinued entry-level Android 4.1 smartphone released in Q4 2013. It features a compact 3.5-inch TFT display (320x480), a Spreadtrum SC7710G chipset with 512MB of RAM, and a 2MP main camera. The device supports Dual SIM, includes a removable 1300 mAh battery, and is available in black or white.

The Karbonn Titanium Hexa is a discontinued Android 4.4.2 smartphone released in April 2014. It features a 5.5-inch Full HD IPS LCD display, a Hexa-core 1.5 GHz CPU, 2GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. It is equipped with a 13MP main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a 2050 mAh Li-Po battery, with an approximate launch price of 200 EUR.

The QMobile E1 is a discontinued Android 6.0 smartphone released in January 2017. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD (720x1280), a Mediatek MT6735P quad-core processor, 3GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes an 8MP main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a large 4000mAh removable Li-Po battery.

The Garmin-Asus nuvifone A50 is a discontinued Android 2.1 smartphone released in 2010. It features a 3.5-inch TFT display, a Qualcomm Snapdragon S1 chipset with a 600 MHz CPU, and 256MB of RAM with 4GB of internal storage. Key specifications include a 3.15 MP main camera, Garmin navigation, and a removable 1150 mAh battery.

The Infinix Note 30 5G, released in May 2023, is a 5G-enabled smartphone featuring a large 6.78-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and a Mediatek Dimensity 6080 chipset. It offers memory options of up to 256GB storage and 8GB RAM, powered by a 5000mAh battery with 45W fast charging. Key highlights include a high-resolution 108MP main camera, JBL-tuned dual speakers, and a side-mounted fingerprint sensor.

The Asus ROG Phone 6 Diablo Immortal Edition is a high-end gaming smartphone released in November 2022. It features a 6.78-inch 165Hz AMOLED display, a Snapdragon 8+ Gen 1 chipset, 16GB of RAM, and 512GB of storage. Key gaming features include pressure-sensitive triggers, an illuminated RGB logo, and dual USB Type-C ports. It is powered by a large 6000mAh battery with 65W fast charging and includes a 50MP triple camera system.

The Samsung Galaxy F34, released in August 2023, is a 5G-enabled smartphone featuring a 6.5-inch 120Hz Super AMOLED display and a large 6000 mAh battery with 25W wired charging. It is powered by the Exynos 1280 chipset, offering 128GB of storage with 6GB or 8GB of RAM. Its camera system includes a 50MP main wide lens with OIS and a 13MP selfie camera. Priced at approximately 210 EUR, it runs Android 13 and includes NFC and a 3.5mm headphone jack.

The Micromax In 2b is an affordable Android 11 smartphone (approx. 90 EUR) released in August 2021. It features a 6.52-inch IPS LCD display, a Unisoc T610 octa-core chipset, and options for 4GB or 6GB of RAM with 64GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes a 5000mAh battery with 10W charging, a 13MP dual main camera, and a 5MP selfie camera.

The Philips S396 is a discontinued Android 5.1 smartphone released in June 2015. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), a quad-core 1.0 GHz CPU, 1GB of RAM, and 8GB of internal storage. It is equipped with 8MP front and rear cameras, a removable 2300 mAh battery, and supports LTE connectivity.

The Prestigio MultiPhone 7600 Duo is a discontinued dual-SIM Android 4.2 smartphone released in Q1 2014. It features a large 6.0-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), a quad-core 1.5 GHz CPU, 1GB of RAM, and 4GB of internal storage. For photography, it is equipped with a 13MP main camera and an 8MP selfie camera. It is powered by a removable 2600 mAh battery and includes NFC and FM radio capabilities, with an original price of approximately 270 EUR.

The Cubot Tab KingKong is a rugged Android 13 tablet released in August 2023, featuring IP68/IP69K dust and water resistance. It sports a 10.1-inch IPS LCD (1200x1920), is powered by a Mediatek MT8788 octa-core chipset with 8GB RAM and 256GB storage, and is equipped with a massive 10,600 mAh battery with 18W charging. Connectivity includes LTE, Wi-Fi, and USB Type-C, while cameras consist of a 16MP main and 8MP selfie lens.

Released in September 2023, the Honor V Purse is a 5G foldable smartphone featuring a 7.71-inch 90Hz OLED main display and a 6.45-inch cover display, both with 1600 nits peak brightness. It is powered by a Snapdragon 778G 5G chipset with 16GB of RAM and up to 512GB of storage. The device includes a 50MP dual main camera, a 4500mAh battery with 35W wired charging, and is priced at approximately 770 EUR.

The Archos Oxygen 68XL is an affordable Android 9.0 smartphone released in May 2019, priced at approximately 150 EUR. It features a massive 6.85-inch IPS LCD display, a long-lasting 5000 mAh battery, and is powered by a Helio P22 chipset with 3GB RAM and 32GB storage. It includes a dual rear camera (8MP main), a 5MP selfie camera, and a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor.

The O2 XDA Comet is a discontinued Windows Mobile 6.0 Professional smartphone released in September 2007. It features a 2.7-inch resistive touchscreen, an Intel XScale 620 MHz CPU, 64MB RAM, and 1GB ROM with miniSD expansion. It is equipped with a 2MP main camera, a VGA front camera, and supports HSPA, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth 1.2. The device is powered by a removable 1350mAh Li-Ion battery.

Released in October 2023, the Oppo A79 is a 5G-enabled smartphone featuring a 6.72-inch 90Hz IPS LCD display and a Mediatek Dimensity 6020 chipset. It comes with 8GB RAM, 128GB of UFS 2.2 storage, and a 5000 mAh battery supporting 33W fast charging. Key features include a 50MP dual main camera, stereo speakers, IPX4 splash resistance, and a side-mounted fingerprint sensor.

The ZTE Blade A73 5G is an affordable Android 13 smartphone released in September 2023. It features a 6.52-inch 90Hz IPS LCD display, a Unisoc T760 chipset, 4GB of RAM, and 128GB of internal storage. Key hardware includes a 50MP dual main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a large 5000 mAh battery. It supports 5G connectivity and is priced around €129.

The Sagem Puma Phone is a discontinued 2010 device featuring a 2.8-inch resistive touchscreen, a 3.15 MP main camera, and a removable 880 mAh battery. Notably designed for fitness, it includes an integrated solar cell, a pedometer, and bike/run trackers. It supports GSM/HSPA networks, Bluetooth 2.1, and GPS, and was priced at approximately 400 EUR.

The Cat S52 is a rugged smartphone released in November 2019, featuring IP68 water/dust resistance, MIL-STD-810G compliance, and drop-to-concrete resistance up to 1.5m. It is equipped with a 5.65-inch IPS LCD (720x1440), a Mediatek Helio P35 chipset, 4GB RAM, and 64GB of storage. Key hardware includes a 12MP main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a 3100 mAh battery with 10W charging, priced at approximately 440 EUR.

The Oppo Reno11 (China) is a 5G smartphone announced in November 2023, priced at approximately 320 EUR. It features a 6.7-inch 120Hz OLED display, a Mediatek Dimensity 8200 chipset, and up to 12GB RAM with up to 512GB of internal storage. Its camera system includes a triple main setup (50MP wide, 32MP telephoto, 8MP ultrawide) and a 32MP selfie camera, both supporting 4K video. The device is powered by a 4800 mAh battery with 67W fast charging and runs Android 14.

The Panasonic P85 Nxt is a budget-friendly Android 7.1.2 smartphone released in November 2018. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display with Gorilla Glass, a Snapdragon 210 quad-core processor, 2GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. Notable highlights include a large 4000 mAh battery, dual SIM support, and a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor, priced at approximately 7000 INR.

The BenQ-Siemens EF61 is a discontinued GSM mobile phone released in July 2006. It features a dual-display setup (primary 128x160 TFT and external 96x64 CSTN), a 1.3 MP main camera, and a dedicated microSD slot. Key connectivity includes Bluetooth and USB 1.1, while the device is powered by a removable 750 mAh Li-Ion battery offering up to 3 hours of talk time.

The Alcatel 1B (2022) is an affordable entry-level smartphone priced around 100 EUR. It features a 5.5-inch TFT LCD display, runs Android 11 (Go edition) on a Mediatek Helio A22 chipset, and is equipped with 2GB RAM and 32GB of internal storage. It includes an 8MP main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a removable 3000 mAh battery.

The T-Mobile Revvlry, released in July 2019, is an entry-level Android 9.0 smartphone priced around 180 EUR. It features a 5.7-inch IPS LCD display, a Snapdragon 632 chipset, 3GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage. It is equipped with a 13MP main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a 3000 mAh battery with 10W wired charging. Notable features include splash resistance, a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor, and a USB Type-C port.

The Honor X50i+, released in November 2023, is a 5G smartphone featuring a 6.7-inch 90Hz AMOLED display with a peak brightness of 2000 nits. It is powered by a Mediatek Dimensity 6080 chipset, 12GB of RAM, and storage options up to 512GB. Notable hardware includes a 108MP main camera, a 4500mAh battery with 35W wired charging, and a slim 6.8mm profile, priced at approximately 210 EUR.

The Vodafone Smart N10, released in May 2019, is an entry-level Android 9.0 smartphone priced at approximately 150 EUR. It features a 5.67-inch IPS LCD display, a Mediatek Helio A22 quad-core chipset, 2GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. The device is equipped with a 13MP main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a 2920 mAh battery.

The Philips PH1 is an affordable Android smartphone released in December 2021, priced at approximately 70 EUR. It features a 6.51-inch IPS LCD display, a Unisoc Tiger T310 chipset with 4GB of RAM, and storage options ranging from 32GB to 128GB. The device is equipped with a 13MP dual main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a 4700mAh battery, and it supports LTE connectivity and USB Type-C.

The Sony Ericsson Xperia active is a discontinued, rugged Android smartphone released in October 2011. It features a compact 3.0-inch display, IP67 dust/water resistance, and a built-in flashlight. Powered by a Snapdragon S2 chipset with 512MB RAM, it includes a 5MP main camera capable of 720p video, a removable 1200 mAh battery, and a dedicated microSDHC slot.

The Sharp Aquos V6 is an entry-level Android 12 smartphone released in February 2022, priced at approximately 130 EUR. It features a 6.52-inch IPS LCD (720x1600), a Mediatek Helio G25 chipset, 3GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage. Key highlights include a large 5000 mAh battery with USB Power Delivery 3.0, a triple rear camera setup (13MP main), and NFC support.

The OnePlus Nord CE 3 Lite is an affordable 5G smartphone released in April 2023, featuring a 6.72-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and a Snapdragon 695 5G chipset. It is equipped with 8GB of RAM and up to 256GB of storage, highlighted by a high-resolution 108MP main camera and a large 5000mAh battery with fast 67W wired charging. The device runs Android 13 and is priced around $267.50.

The Archos Oxygen 63 is an affordable Android 9.0 smartphone released in May 2019, priced at approximately 130 EUR. It features a 6.26-inch IPS LCD display (720x1520), a Unisoc SC9863 octa-core chipset, 4GB of RAM, and 64GB of internal storage. The device includes a dual rear camera (8MP main), a 5MP selfie camera, a removable 3000mAh battery, and a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor.

The Parla Mist is a discontinued triple-SIM GSM phone released in late 2012. It features a QWERTY keyboard, a 1.8-inch TFT display, and a VGA main camera with LED flash. Key specifications include 32MB of RAM, a dedicated microSDHC slot, Bluetooth 2.1, and a removable 700 mAh battery providing up to 6 hours of talk time.

The Allview Soul X6 Xtreme, released in April 2019, is an Android 9.0 smartphone featuring a 6.39-inch AMOLED display with Gorilla Glass 5 and an under-display fingerprint sensor. It is powered by a Mediatek Helio P70 chipset with 4GB RAM and 64GB of storage. Notable hardware includes a dual 16MP main camera, a motorized pop-up 8MP selfie camera, and a 3450 mAh battery supporting 18W wired and wireless charging. It was priced at approximately 360 EUR.

The i-mobile Hitz 2206 is a discontinued silver Dual SIM feature phone released in 2010. It features a QWERTY keyboard, a 220x176 pixel TFT display, a 0.3MP main camera, and a removable 1000 mAh battery offering up to 667 hours of standby time. Connectivity is basic, including GSM network support and an FM radio, but it lacks Bluetooth, WLAN, and a 3.5mm jack.

The Eten glofiish X900 is a discontinued Windows Mobile 6.1 Professional smartphone released in June 2008. It features a 2.8-inch resistive touchscreen (480x640 pixels), a 533 MHz Samsung CPU, 128MB RAM, and a 3.15 MP main camera. Connectivity includes HSPA, Wi-Fi, and GPS, and it is powered by a removable 1300mAh Li-Ion battery.

The Yezz Max 2 Ultra is an Android 11 smartphone released in July 2022. It features a 6.1-inch IPS LCD display (600x1280), a Mediatek MT6737M quad-core processor, 2GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage. For photography, it has a 13MP dual main camera and an 8MP selfie camera. The device is powered by a removable 3000 mAh battery and includes a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor.

The Tel.Me. T939 was a Windows PocketPC 2003 Phone edition device announced in Q1 2004, though it was ultimately cancelled. It featured a 160x240 pixel resistive touchscreen, a 2MP autofocus camera with LED flash, and an 800mAh removable Li-Ion battery, but lacked Bluetooth, WLAN, and a 3.5mm jack.

The LG K52, released in November 2020, is a budget-friendly smartphone featuring a 6.6-inch IPS LCD display (720x1600) and is MIL-STD-810G compliant. It is powered by a Mediatek Helio P35 chipset with 4GB RAM and 64GB of internal storage. Key highlights include a versatile quad-camera system led by a 48MP main sensor, a 13MP selfie camera, and a 4000mAh battery. It runs Android 10 and includes NFC, a side-mounted fingerprint sensor, and USB Type-C 3.1 connectivity.

The Wind DUO 2000 is a discontinued dual-SIM GSM phone announced in March 2007. It features a compact 1.5-inch OLED display, Bluetooth 1.2, and a removable 1660 mAh battery. The device lacks a camera, WLAN, and a 3.5mm jack, but includes an MP3 player and Java support.

The Modu Sport jacket is a discontinued 2009 GSM device featuring a small 1.3-inch AMOLED display and 1.6GB of internal memory. Designed for fitness, it includes a built-in pedometer, sport applications, and an MP3 player, powered by a removable 530 mAh battery. It lacks a camera, WLAN, and 3.5mm jack, but supports Bluetooth 1.2.

Released in September 2023, the Doogee T10Plus is an Android 13 tablet featuring a 10.5-inch IPS LCD display (1200 x 1920 pixels) and stylus support. It is powered by a Unisoc T606 octa-core chipset with 8GB of RAM and 256GB of internal storage. Key hardware includes a 13MP dual main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, quad stereo speakers, and a large 8250 mAh battery with 18W wired charging. The device is built with an aluminum frame and back and supports LTE connectivity.

The Sony Ericsson Xperia mini pro is a discontinued compact smartphone released in August 2011. It features a 3.0-inch LED-backlit LCD, a physical QWERTY keyboard, and runs on Android (up to Ice Cream Sandwich) powered by a 1.0 GHz Snapdragon S2 chipset. Key specs include 512MB RAM, a 5MP main camera with 720p video, and a removable 1200 mAh battery.

The Nvidia Shield is a discontinued 8.0-inch Android tablet released in July 2014. It features a 1920 x 1200 IPS LCD display, is powered by an Nvidia Tegra K1 chipset with a quad-core 2.2 GHz CPU, and includes 2GB of RAM and 16GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes stereo speakers with a dual bass reflex port, an HDMI port, and a 5400 mAh battery.

Released in September 2023, the Doogee S41 Pro is a rugged Android 12 smartphone featuring IP68/IP69K and MIL-STD-810H certification for water, dust, and drop resistance. It is powered by a Helio A22 chipset with 4GB RAM and 64GB storage, and features a 5.5-inch 720p IPS LCD display. Key highlights include a massive 6300 mAh battery, a 13MP triple main camera, and a durable build weighing 262g.

The Posh Volt Max LTE L640 is a discontinued Android 5.1 smartphone released in June 2016. It features a large 6.44-inch IPS LCD display, an octa-core 1.3 GHz processor, 3GB of RAM, and storage options of 32GB or 64GB. Notable highlights include a massive 6000 mAh non-removable battery and a 13MP main camera, though it lacks an SD card slot and NFC.

The VK Mobile VK4000 is a discontinued GSM phone announced in March 2006. It is a compact, lightweight device (79g) featuring a 1.8-inch TFT display, 128MB of internal memory, and a VGA main camera. Key features include Bluetooth, Java support, and an MP3/AAC player, powered by a removable 800mAh Li-Ion battery. It was priced at approximately 60 EUR.

The itel A70, released in November 2023, is an entry-level Android 13 (Go edition) smartphone featuring a 6.6-inch IPS LCD display and a Unisoc T603 octa-core chipset. It offers storage options up to 256GB with up to 4GB of RAM, a 13MP dual main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a long-lasting 5000mAh battery with 10W wired charging.

The Sendo X is a discontinued GSM mobile phone released in Q4 2003. It features a 2.2-inch TFT display, runs on Symbian 6.1 with a 120 MHz ARM925T CPU, and includes 32MB of internal memory with an MMC/SD card slot. Key specs include a VGA main camera with LED flash, Bluetooth 1.1, an infrared port, and a removable 1050 mAh battery providing up to 7 hours of talk time.

The Realme Narzo 60x is a 5G-enabled smartphone released in September 2023, featuring a 6.72-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and a Mediatek Dimensity 6100+ chipset. It comes with 128GB of storage and 4GB or 6GB of RAM, a 50MP dual main camera, and an 8MP selfie camera. Powering the device is a 5000mAh battery with 33W fast charging, priced at ₹12,999.

The LG K92 5G is a 5G-enabled Android 10 smartphone released in November 2020. It features a large 6.7-inch IPS LCD display (1080 x 2400), a Snapdragon 690 5G chipset, 6GB of RAM, and 128GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes a 64MP quad-camera system capable of 4K video, a 16MP selfie camera, stereo speakers, and a 4000 mAh battery with Quick Charge 4.0. It is priced at approximately 340 EUR.

The WND Wind Van Gogh 2100 was a compact GSM phone announced in October 2007 but ultimately cancelled. It featured a 1.8-inch TFT display, a 2 MP main camera, and 128MB of internal storage. Notable features included a detachable Bluetooth earpiece, FM radio, and a removable 820 mAh battery.

The Spice Smart Flo 358 (Mi-358) is a discontinued entry-level Android 4.4.2 smartphone released in October 2014. It features a 3.5-inch TFT display (320x480), a dual-core 1.3 GHz processor, and minimal memory with 256MB RAM and 512MB internal storage. The device is equipped with a 2MP main camera, a 1.3MP selfie camera, dual SIM support, and is powered by a 1450 mAh Li-Ion battery.

The Motorola Edge (2023) is a 5G smartphone released in October 2023, priced at $349.99. It features a 6.6-inch 144Hz P-OLED display, a MediaTek Dimensity 7030 chipset, 8GB of RAM, and 256GB of storage. Notable specs include an IP68 rating, a 50MP main camera with OIS, and a 4400 mAh battery supporting 68W wired and 15W wireless charging.

The Huawei Watch Ultimate Design is a luxury smartwatch released in October 2023, featuring a premium build with an 18k gold bezel and crown, sapphire crystal, and a ceramic back. It boasts a 1.5-inch LTPO AMOLED display, runs HarmonyOS 4.0, and is highly durable with 100m water resistance (10 ATM) and IP68 certification. Key features include extensive health sensors, BDS satellite messaging, a 530 mAh battery with wireless charging, and a high price point of approximately 4,360 EUR.

The Plum Optimax 13 is a budget 10.1-inch Android 8.0 (Go edition) tablet released in early 2019. It features a 1280x800 IPS LCD display, a Spreadtrum SC7731 quad-core processor, 1GB of RAM, and 8GB of internal storage. It is equipped with a 5MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a 4700 mAh battery, supporting Dual SIM connectivity.

The Innostream INNO 36 is a compact GSM phone announced in Q2 2005 but ultimately cancelled. It features a primary 128x143 TFT display, a secondary external STN screen, a VGA main camera, and an infrared port. The device weighs 76g and is powered by a removable 920mAh Li-Ion battery.

The Toshiba Excite 10 SE is a discontinued 10.1-inch Android 4.1 tablet released in December 2012. It features an Nvidia Tegra 3 quad-core processor, 1GB of RAM, 16GB of internal storage, and a 1280x800 resolution LED-backlit IPS LCD. The device lacks cellular connectivity but includes Wi-Fi, Bluetooth 3.0, and GPS, and was priced at approximately 250 EUR.

The Unnecto Neo V is a discontinued Android 5.1 smartphone released in July 2016. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (480x854), a quad-core 1.3 GHz CPU, 1GB of RAM, and 8GB of internal storage. It is equipped with an 8MP main camera, a 1.3MP selfie camera, stereo speakers, and a removable 2000 mAh battery.

The Posh Optima LTE L530 is a discontinued Android 5.1 smartphone released in April 2016. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), an octa-core 1.3 GHz processor, 2GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. Notably, it is equipped with matching 13MP cameras on both the front and back and is powered by a non-removable 3000 mAh battery.

The Dell Streak 7 Wi-Fi is a discontinued 7-inch Android tablet released in April 2011. It features a 480x800 TFT display with Gorilla Glass, an Nvidia Tegra 2 dual-core processor, 16GB of internal storage, and a 2780 mAh battery. It includes a 5MP main camera and 1.3MP selfie camera, but lacks cellular connectivity.

The Cubot Note 21 is an affordable Android 13 smartphone released in September 2023, priced around $79.99. It features a 6.56-inch 90Hz IPS LCD, a Unisoc T606 octa-core chipset, 6GB of RAM, and 128GB of storage. Key highlights include a 50MP main camera and a large 5200 mAh battery with 10W wired charging.

The MWg Atom Life is a discontinued Windows Mobile 6 Professional smartphone announced in January 2007. It features a 2.7-inch resistive touchscreen, an Intel XScale 624 MHz CPU, 64MB RAM, and a 2MP main camera. Connectivity includes HSPA, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth 1.2, and an infrared port, all powered by a removable 1530 mAh battery.

The itel Smart Watch 2, released in 2023, features a 1.8-inch IPS LCD display (240x286 pixels) and a plastic build with IP68 water resistance. It runs on a proprietary OS, lacks cellular connectivity, and includes health sensors for heart rate, SpO2, and acceleration. The device is powered by a 230 mAh Li-Ion battery and supports Bluetooth 5.1.

The Sony Ericsson WT18i is a discontinued Android-based (OMS 2.5) smartphone released in Q3 2011. It features a compact 3.2-inch TFT display, an 806 MHz Marvell PXA 920 CPU, and limited internal memory of 150MB, expandable via microSDHC. Key specs include a 3.15 MP main camera, a 1200 mAh removable battery, and a dedicated Walkman player. It was priced at approximately 230 EUR.

The VK Mobile VK7000 is a GSM/UMTS device announced in March 2006, though it was ultimately cancelled. It features a 1.8-inch TFT display (176x220 pixels), a 1.3MP main camera, a VGA selfie camera, and an 800mAh removable Li-Ion battery. Other notable specs include Bluetooth, USB 2.0, and Java support, but it lacks Wi-Fi and a 3.5mm headphone jack.

The Panasonic Eluga X1 Pro, released in October 2018, is a mid-range smartphone featuring a 6.18-inch IPS LCD display (1080 x 2246 pixels) and a premium build with glass and a stainless steel frame. It is powered by a Helio P60 chipset with options for 4GB or 6GB of RAM and up to 128GB of storage. Notable features include a 16MP dual rear camera, a 16MP selfie camera, infrared face ID, and wireless charging, all supported by a 3000 mAh battery. It launched at approximately 350 EUR.

The AT&T Quickfire is a discontinued QWERTY keyboard phone released in November 2008. It features a 2.8-inch TFT display (240x320 pixels), a 1.3 MP main camera, and a removable 1200 mAh battery. Connectivity includes GSM/HSPA, Bluetooth 1.2, and GPS, though it lacks WLAN, a 3.5mm jack, and a selfie camera.

The Fairphone 4, released in October 2021, is a 5G-enabled smartphone featuring a 6.3-inch IPS LCD display and a Snapdragon 750G 5G chipset. It offers memory configurations of 128GB/6GB or 256GB/8GB RAM and is equipped with a dual 48MP main camera system and a 25MP selfie camera. Notable for its durability and sustainability, it is IP54 rated, MIL-STD-810G compliant, and features a removable 3905 mAh battery with a standard 5-year warranty.

The Apple iPhone 15 Plus, released in September 2023, features a 6.7-inch Super Retina XDR OLED display and is powered by the A16 Bionic chipset with 6GB of RAM and storage options up to 512GB. It is equipped with a dual-camera system headlined by a 48MP wide lens and a 12MP ultrawide lens, supporting 4K video recording. The device includes a 4383 mAh battery with MagSafe wireless charging, USB Type-C connectivity, and IP68 water resistance. It is available in five colors and is priced starting at approximately $929.99.

The Alcatel 1L Pro (2021) is an entry-level smartphone released in Q2 2021, priced at approximately 110 EUR. It features a 6.1-inch IPS LCD display (720x1560), is powered by a Unisoc SC9863A octa-core chipset with 2GB RAM and 32GB storage, and runs Android 11 (Go edition). It is equipped with a 13MP dual rear camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a 3000mAh battery.

The verykool s5036 Apollo is an entry-level Android 7.0 smartphone released in February 2018. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display, a Mediatek MT6580M quad-core processor, 1GB of RAM, and 8GB of internal storage. Key features include a dual rear camera (8MP main and 2MP depth), a 5MP selfie camera, a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor, and a removable 2000 mAh battery.

The BenQ C36 is a discontinued GSM mobile phone released in April 2008. It features a compact 2.0-inch TFT display, a 1.3 MP main camera, and a removable 640 mAh battery providing up to 180 hours of standby time. Key features include a microSD slot, FM radio, and an MP3 player with dedicated music keys, though it lacks Bluetooth, WLAN, and a selfie camera.

The Vodafone Smart V8 is a discontinued Android 7.1.1 smartphone released in June 2017. It features a 5.5-inch 1080p IPS LCD display, a Snapdragon 435 octa-core processor, 3GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage. Key specifications include a 16MP main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor, NFC support, and a non-removable 3000 mAh battery with 10W charging. It was priced at approximately 230 EUR.

The Acer Iconia Tab 10 A3-A40 is a discontinued 10.1-inch Android 6.0 tablet released in June 2016. It features a 1920 x 1200 IPS LCD display, a Mediatek MT8163A quad-core processor, 2GB of RAM, and storage options ranging from 16GB to 64GB. Key hardware includes a 6100 mAh battery, quad stereo speakers, a 5MP main camera, and a microUSB 2.0 port, though it lacks cellular connectivity.

The Maxwest Gravity 5 LTE is a discontinued Android 5.1 smartphone released in February 2016. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), a Snapdragon 210 quad-core processor, 1GB of RAM, and 8GB of internal storage. It is equipped with an 8MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a removable 2200 mAh battery.

The TCL Ion X is an entry-level Android smartphone released in January 2023, priced at approximately 100 EUR. It features a 6.0-inch IPS LCD display (720x1440), a Mediatek Helio G25 octa-core chipset, 3GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage. It is equipped with a 13MP main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a removable 3000 mAh battery with 10W wired charging.

The Spice Smart Flo 503 (Mi-503) is a discontinued Android 4.4.2 smartphone released in August 2014. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (480x854 pixels), a dual-core 1.3 GHz processor, and minimal memory with 512MB RAM and 4GB internal storage. It is equipped with an 8MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a 2000mAh battery.

The Sony Ericsson Xperia Arc S is a discontinued Android smartphone released in September 2011. It features a 4.2-inch LED-backlit LCD display, a 1.4 GHz Scorpion CPU (Snapdragon S2), 512MB of RAM, and an 8MP main camera capable of 720p video. It is powered by a removable 1500 mAh battery and was originally priced around 210 EUR.

Released in November 2023, the Honor 100 is a 5G smartphone featuring a 6.7-inch 120Hz OLED display with a peak brightness of 2600 nits. It is powered by the Snapdragon 7 Gen 3 chipset, offering up to 16GB of RAM and 512GB of storage. The device boasts a 50MP main camera with OIS and a 50MP selfie camera, both supporting 4K video. It includes a 5000 mAh battery with fast 100W wired charging and is priced at approximately 320 EUR.

The Tecno Pova 5 is an Android 13 smartphone featuring a large 6.78-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and is powered by the Mediatek Helio G99 chipset with 8GB of RAM and up to 256GB of storage. Key highlights include a massive 6000mAh battery with 45W fast charging, a 50MP main camera, and stereo speakers, all priced at ₹11,999.

The Sagem my721z is a discontinued 2G GSM mobile phone released in February 2008. It features a 2.2-inch TFT display, a 3.15 MP main camera, and a 1000mAh removable Li-Ion battery. Other notable specs include Bluetooth, Stereo FM radio, a dedicated microSD slot, and a compact silver body weighing 125g.

The BLU G73, released in June 2023 for approximately 140 EUR, is an Android 13 smartphone featuring a large 6.8-inch 90Hz IPS LCD display. It is powered by an octa-core CPU with 6GB RAM and 128GB of internal storage. Key hardware includes a 50MP triple main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a substantial 5000mAh battery with 18W wired charging. The device is built with an eco-leather back and includes a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor.

The BlackBerry Keyone, released in April 2017, is an Android-based smartphone featuring a signature capacitive QWERTY keyboard and a 4.5-inch IPS LCD display. It is powered by a Snapdragon 625 chipset with options for 3GB or 4GB of RAM and 32GB or 64GB of storage. Key hardware includes a 12MP main camera capable of 4K video, a 3500mAh battery with 18W fast charging, and a front-mounted fingerprint sensor.

Released in June 2021, the Gionee Smartwatch 7 is a lightweight (35g), IP67-rated wearable priced at approximately 50 EUR. It features a 1.3-inch TFT LCD (240x240 pixels), a 130 mAh battery, and health sensors for heart rate, SpO2, and acceleration, though it lacks cellular connectivity, Wi-Fi, and a camera.

The Coolpad Cool 10A, released in March 2021, is an entry-level Android 11 smartphone priced at approximately 90 EUR. It features a 6.52-inch IPS LCD display, a Unisoc SC9863A octa-core chipset, 2GB of RAM, and 64GB of internal storage. The device is equipped with a 13MP dual main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a 4000mAh battery with 10W wired charging.

Released in December 2022, the Wiko 5G is a HarmonyOS smartphone featuring a 6.78-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and a Snapdragon 695 5G chipset. It offers 8GB of RAM with storage options of 128GB or 256GB, and is highlighted by a 108MP quad-camera system. The device includes a 4000mAh battery with fast 66W wired charging and is priced at approximately 270 EUR.

The Philips I908 is a discontinued dual-SIM smartphone released in August 2015. It features a 5.0-inch Full HD IPS LCD display, an octa-core 1.7 GHz CPU running Android 4.4.2, 2GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. It is equipped with a 13MP main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a 3000mAh Li-Ion battery.

The HP Elite x3 is a discontinued Windows 10 smartphone released in August 2016. It features a 5.96-inch AMOLED display (1440x2560), a Snapdragon 820 processor, 4GB RAM, and 64GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes a 16MP main camera, Bang & Olufsen tuned stereo speakers, a 4150 mAh battery with wireless charging, and IP67 dust/water resistance. It also supports Microsoft Continuum and includes both iris and fingerprint scanners.

The Infinix Smart 8, released in November 2023, is an affordable smartphone (approx. 100 EUR) featuring a 6.6-inch 90Hz IPS LCD display. It is powered by a Unisoc T606 chipset with 8GB RAM and 128GB internal storage, running Android 13. Notable hardware includes a 5000mAh battery with 10W charging, a 13MP dual main camera, and an 8MP selfie camera.

The BLU Studio X5 Max is an affordable Android 11 (Go edition) smartphone released in July 2022, priced at approximately 50 EUR. It features a 5.7-inch IPS LCD display, a quad-core 1.3 GHz CPU, 2GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage. The device is equipped with a 5MP dual main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a removable 2700 mAh battery.

The Oukitel RT6 is a rugged 10.1-inch Android 13 tablet released in July 2023, priced at approximately 350 EUR. It is built for durability with IP68/IP69K water/dust resistance, MIL-STD-810H compliance, and drop resistance. Key specifications include a Mediatek MT8788 chipset, 8GB RAM, 256GB UFS 2.1 storage, and a massive 20,000 mAh battery with 33W wired and reverse charging. It features 16MP front and rear cameras and a 1200 x 1920 resolution IPS LCD.

The LeEco Le 2, released in April 2016, is a discontinued Android 6.0 smartphone featuring a 5.5-inch 1080p IPS LCD display. It is powered by either a Helio X20 or Snapdragon 652 chipset with 3GB of RAM and 32GB or 64GB of internal storage. Key features include a 16MP main camera capable of 4K video, an 8MP selfie camera, a 3000mAh battery with 18W fast charging, and a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor.

The Kyocera Hydro Life is a discontinued rugged smartphone released in August 2014. It features a 4.5-inch IPS LCD, Snapdragon 200 chipset, 1.5GB RAM, and 8GB of internal storage. Notably, it is highly durable with IP57 dust/water resistance and MIL-STD-810G compliance. It is equipped with a 5MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a removable 2000 mAh battery.

The Icemobile Prime 5.5 is a discontinued Dual SIM Android 4.4.2 (KitKat) smartphone released in August 2014. It features a 5.46-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), a quad-core 1.3 GHz CPU, 1GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. It is equipped with an 8MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a removable 2250 mAh battery.

Released in September 2023, the Blackview A96 is an affordable smartphone priced at approximately $161.69. It features a 6.5-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and is powered by a Mediatek Helio G99 chipset with options for 8GB or 12GB of RAM and 256GB of UFS 2.2 storage. The device includes a dual main camera setup (48MP wide and 8MP ultrawide), a 16MP selfie camera, and a 4380 mAh battery with 18W wired charging. It runs Android 13 and supports NFC and dual SIMs.

The Energizer U680S is an entry-level Android 11 smartphone released in Q3 2021, priced around 120 EUR. Its standout feature is a massive 7000 mAh battery, paired with a large 6.8-inch IPS LCD display. It is powered by a Unisoc SC9863A octa-core chipset with 2GB RAM and 32GB storage, and features a triple main camera setup led by a 13MP sensor.

The Ericsson A2628 is a discontinued GSM mobile phone announced in 2001. It features a monochrome 4-line graphic display, a Mini-SIM slot, and basic functionality including SMS, WAP 1.1, and three built-in games. The device lacks a camera, Bluetooth, and Wi-Fi, and it is powered by a removable NiMH battery providing up to 11 hours of talk time with the high-capacity version.

The Lava Yuva 2 Pro, released in February 2023, is an affordable Android 12 smartphone priced at ₹6,620. It features a 6.52-inch IPS LCD display, a Mediatek Helio G37 chipset, 4GB of RAM, and 64GB of internal storage. Key hardware includes a triple main camera (13MP wide), a 5MP selfie camera, and a large 5000 mAh battery with 10W wired charging.

The Meizu 18s Pro is a high-end 5G smartphone featuring a 6.7-inch 120Hz Super AMOLED display with QHD+ resolution. It is powered by the Snapdragon 888+ 5G chipset, available with up to 12GB RAM and 256GB UFS 3.1 storage. The device boasts a versatile triple camera system led by a 50MP main sensor and a 44MP selfie camera. It is equipped with a 4500 mAh battery supporting 40W wired and wireless charging, and includes an ultrasonic under-display fingerprint sensor.

The Spice Smart Flo 508 (Mi-508) is a discontinued Android 4.4.2 smartphone released in August 2014. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (480 x 854 pixels), a dual-core 1.3 GHz CPU, and limited memory with 512MB RAM and 4GB internal storage. It is equipped with an 8MP main camera and a 2MP selfie camera, and is powered by a 2000mAh Li-Ion battery.

The VK Mobile VK300 is a discontinued GSM phone announced in July 2005. It is a compact, lightweight device (75g) featuring a 1.8-inch TFT display and a secondary external STN display. It lacks a camera, Bluetooth, and USB connectivity, relying on WAP 1.2.1 for browsing. It is powered by a removable 720 mAh Li-Ion battery providing up to 3 hours of talk time.
